title: Use explicit destructuring in Claude event payload mapping (bridge.ts)
analysis:
The `subscribeToClaudeEvents` function in `gui/src/bridge.ts` (lines 153-186) maps 7 Tauri events to `OutboundMessage` discriminated-union variants. Six of the seven use the pattern `{ type: 'X', ...event.payload } as OutboundMessage`, which has three problems: (1) the `as OutboundMessage` cast suppresses TS structural verification, (2) the spread comes AFTER the hardcoded `type` so any `type` field in the payload silently overrides it, and (3) the spread allocates a new object per event on the hot streaming path (text_delta arrives at 50-200 Hz).

The fix replaces each spread-cast handler with explicit destructuring: cast `event.payload` to the expected payload shape, then construct the `OutboundMessage` by naming each field. This lets TS verify the union shape, prevents payload `type` override, avoids forwarding unknown fields, and avoids the spread allocation.

The `claude-ready` handler already constructs `{ type: 'ready' }` directly — no change needed there.

Existing tests in `claudeBridge.test.ts` already verify correct mapping for all 7 event types and rollback behavior. New tests will verify the two correctness properties the spread pattern failed to guarantee: (a) payload `type` field doesn't override the mapped type, (b) extra unknown fields in payload are not forwarded.

The `OutboundMessage` type and its constituent interfaces in `gui/sidecar/src/types.ts` are NOT modified — they already define the correct shapes.

steps.description: Add failing test: payload containing a `type` field must not override the mapped event type. Simulate a claude-text-delta event with payload `{ id: 'x', content: 'hi', type: 'WRONG' }` and assert the handler receives `type: 'text_delta'`, not `type: 'WRONG'`. This fails with the current spread pattern because spread properties override earlier keys.

Add failing test: extra unknown fields in payload must not be forwarded to the handler. Simulate a claude-done event with payload `{ id: 'x', _internal: true, debug_ts: 12345 }` and assert the handler receives exactly `{ type: 'done', id: 'x' }` with no extra properties. This fails with the current spread pattern because all payload fields are copied.

Refactor all 6 payload-bearing handlers in `subscribeToClaudeEvents` to use explicit destructuring instead of spread-cast. For each handler: (1) cast `event.payload` to the expected field-shape (e.g. `as { id: string; content: string }`), (2) construct the OutboundMessage with explicit field references (e.g. `{ type: 'text_delta', id: p.id, content: p.content }`). Remove the `as OutboundMessage` casts. The `claude-ready` handler needs no change. All existing tests plus new step-1/step-2 tests must pass.
Run all existing claudeBridge.test.ts tests to verify no regressions: all 7 event type mappings, combined unlisten, and listener rollback on partial failure tests must still pass.

design_decisions.decision: Cast event.payload to expected shape rather than removing all casts
design_decisions.rationale: Tauri's listen() types the event as `{ payload: T }` but the generic T is `Record<string, unknown>` from the EventEntry type. A cast on the payload is necessary since Tauri doesn't know the Rust struct shape. The key improvement is that this cast is on the INPUT (payload fields) rather than the OUTPUT (OutboundMessage), so TS can still verify the constructed object matches the discriminated union.

design_decisions.decision: Keep the EventEntry array pattern instead of switching to individual listen() calls
design_decisions.rationale: The array pattern with sequential registration + rollback is already tested and provides clean error handling. The explicit destructuring change is inside each handler lambda — it doesn't require changing the overall subscription architecture.

design_decisions.decision: Test type-override and extra-field scenarios specifically on representative event types rather than all 6
design_decisions.rationale: The type-override test uses text_delta (most common hot-path event) and the extra-field test uses done (simplest payload). Testing all 6 would be redundant since they all use the same pattern, and the existing per-event-type mapping tests already provide coverage for each variant's fields.

design_decisions.decision: No changes to the OutboundMessage type definitions in sidecar/src/types.ts
design_decisions.rationale: The type definitions are already correct — they define the discriminated union with all required fields. The problem is solely in how bridge.ts constructs instances of these types.

reuse.what: OutboundMessage discriminated union and constituent interfaces
reuse.how: Reference the field names from TextDelta, ThinkingDelta, ToolCall, ToolResult, Done, ErrorMessage, Ready interfaces to know exactly which fields each destructured handler must extract
New tests follow the same capturedHandler pattern: mockListen captures the handler for a specific event name, then invoke it with a crafted payload and assert the handler call args
New tests are added to the existing describe('subscribeToClaudeEvents') block which already has the mocks and mockListen set up
